mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures household consumption momentum, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy raises the equity discount rate and slows credit creation, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, or the mean return in those periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate an aggressive monetary response to inflation that contracts real earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term constructed from CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS achieves a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-increase observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment is already below 5%, a subsequent rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and falling corporate margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months while level < 5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns follow a 0.2pp increase in UNRATE in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units signal robust residential investment and construction employment, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with strong construction activity
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1500, or the mean return in those periods is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm a self-reinforcing expansion of income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansionary regime with positive employment and consumption dynamics
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-increase months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and inventory destocking, which produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of non-overlapping quarters over any 3-year rolling window.
